docs.title: Docs - Fridge Temperature Monitor
docs.overview.title: Project Overview
docs.overview.desc: An IoT system that measures real-time refrigerator temperature, stores it in a cloud DB, and allows monitoring from anywhere via a web app (PWA).
docs.overview.pwaRole: Use like an app in mobile browser
docs.hardware.title: Hardware Guide
docs.hardware.warningVin: VIN must be connected to 3V3. Connecting to 5V will damage the MAX31865!
docs.hardware.assembly1: Check PT100 wires - Identify 2-wire (2 wires) or 3-wire (3 wires)
docs.hardware.assembly2: MAX31865 jumper settings - Set solder jumpers on the back of the board to match PT100 type
docs.hardware.assembly3: PT100 → MAX31865 - Insert PT100 wires into screw terminal and tighten
docs.hardware.assembly4: ESP32 ↔ MAX31865 SPI wiring - Refer to wiring table above
docs.hardware.assembly5: Breadboard layout - Place components and connect jumper wires
docs.hardware.assembly7: USB connection test - Confirm LED lights up
docs.hardware.noSolderInfo: If header pins are not soldered, temporary connections using alligator clips/Dupont connectors are possible (unstable). Recommended: Purchase an inexpensive soldering iron set or visit a maker space.
docs.hardware.installTip1: Place only the PT100 inside the fridge
docs.hardware.installTip2: Route lead wires through the gap under the door gasket
docs.hardware.installTip3: Place ESP32 + MAX31865 outside the fridge
docs.hardware.commonMistakes: Common mistakes: Connecting VIN to 5V (board damage), Swapping SDO↔SDI pin order, Loose PT100 screws (unstable readings)
docs.firmware.title: Firmware Guide
docs.firmware.feature4: Offline buffer (data retention on Wi-Fi disconnect)
docs.firmware.feature5: Send alert flag on temperature anomaly
docs.firmware.compileInfo: The red text "#pragma message: BT: forcing BR/EDR max sync conn eff to 1" is an informational message, not an error. It's a Bluetooth-related warning unrelated to this project.
docs.firmware.compileResult: Normal compile result: Program storage 81%, dynamic memory 15% used
docs.firebase.title: Firebase Setup
docs.firebase.why1: Instant use with Gmail account (no AWS signup needed)
docs.firebase.setup3: Firestore Database → Create in Seoul region, test mode
docs.firebase.dataStructurePre:
temperatures (collection)
  └─ {docId} (document)
      ├─ temperature: 4.5        (number)
      ├─ recorded_at: Timestamp  (timestamp)
      ├─ device_id: "fridge_01"  (string)
      └─ alert: false            (boolean)

metadata (collection)
  └─ cleanup_last (document)
      └─ timestamp: Timestamp    (timestamp)

messages (collection)
  └─ {docId} (document)
      ├─ text: "message"          (string, 1-500 chars)
      ├─ uid: "abc123"            (anonymous auth UID)
      ├─ nickname: "Admin1"        (string, 1-20 chars)
      └─ created_at: Timestamp    (timestamp)
docs.firebase.securityRulesDesc: Switched from test mode to security rules to protect data.
docs.firebase.secRuleCreateVal: Auth + field validation (temperature, recorded_at, device_id, alert)
docs.firebase.secRuleDeleteVal: Auth + only data older than 30 days
docs.firebase.secRuleMsgCreateVal: Auth + field validation (text, uid, nickname, created_at) + length limits
docs.firebase.secRuleMsgDeleteVal: Auth + only messages older than 30 days
docs.firebase.pricingInfo: Unlikely to exceed the free tier for personal projects.
docs.webapp.title: Web App Guide
docs.webapp.feature5: Temperature alert settings (upper/lower limit, browser notification)
docs.webapp.feature9: PWA (add to home screen like an app)
docs.webapp.feature21: Auto-cleanup of data older than 30 days
docs.webapp.feature22: Anonymous chat (Firestore real-time, floating button UI)
docs.webapp.fakeDataPre:
cd test-firebase
npm run send       # Send 24h of fake data
npm run simulate   # Simulate ESP32 real-time
npm run read       # Read stored data + stats
docs.changelog.title: Changelog
docs.changelog.high2: Dynamic Firestore query limits by period
docs.changelog.medium7: Remove page reload on language switch
docs.troubleshoot.title: Troubleshooting
docs.troubleshoot.noPortCause: Cause: CP2102N USB to UART Bridge driver not installed
docs.troubleshoot.noPortStep4: Unplug and reconnect ESP32 USB cable
docs.troubleshoot.compileMsg: Message: #pragma message: BT: forcing BR/EDR max sync conn eff to 1
docs.troubleshoot.compileCause: Cause: Informational message from ESP32 Bluetooth library (warning)
docs.troubleshoot.compileFix: Fix: Can be safely ignored. Compilation succeeded.
docs.troubleshoot.unstable1: Verify PT100 screw terminal screws are tight enough
docs.troubleshoot.unstable2: Verify 2-wire/3-wire jumper settings match PT100 type
docs.troubleshoot.unstable3: Verify SPI wiring (especially SDO↔SDI) is not swapped
docs.troubleshoot.noWebData: No Data Showing in Web App
docs.troubleshoot.noWebData1: Verify data exists in Firestore via Firebase Console
docs.troubleshoot.noWebData2: Verify anonymous auth is enabled in Authentication
docs.troubleshoot.noWebData3: Check browser console (F12) for error messages
docs.troubleshoot.noWebData4: Test: Send fake data with cd test-firebase && npm run send
docs.references.title: References
chat.title: Chat
chat.noMessages: No messages yet. Start the conversation!
docs.firebase.secRulePresenceVal: Auth + own UID only for create/update/delete
docs.firebase.secRuleTypingVal: Auth + own UID only for create/update/delete
docs.changelog.chat21: Anonymous chat (Firestore real-time, floating button UI)
docs.changelog.chat22: Unread message badge (red badge on FAB button)
docs.changelog.chat23: Duplicate send prevention (disable button during send)
docs.changelog.chat24: Smart auto-scroll (preserve scroll when reading older messages)
docs.changelog.chat25: Date separator (divider between different day messages)
docs.changelog.chat29: Typing indicator (shows when someone is typing)
chat.sendFailed: Failed to send message. Please try again.
app.pwa.installMsg: Add this app to your home screen!
app.noDataSub: Data will appear here once the sensor starts sending
docs.changelog.v2_1: Fix ESP32 timestamp timezone mismatch (KST→UTC unified)
docs.changelog.v2_2: Alert history respects temperature unit setting (°C/°F)
docs.changelog.v2_3: Data cleanup now loops to delete all old records
docs.changelog.v2_4: Remove redundant device discovery listener (cost savings)
docs.changelog.v2_5: Improved chat unread message count accuracy
docs.changelog.v2_6: Settings panel DOM rendering performance improvement
docs.changelog.v2_7: Show date on X-axis for 3D/7D chart views
docs.changelog.v22_1: Fix chart resize losing selected period value
docs.changelog.v22_2: Fix system theme auto-detection logic (existing user compatibility)
docs.changelog.v22_3: Fix history list time display format (remove date)
docs.changelog.v22_4: Fix alert sound AudioContext memory leak
docs.changelog.v22_5: Optimize stat card icon repeated rendering
